# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Application, Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v12.6.0/mod.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const router = new Router();

router.get("/search", async (ctx) => {
  const { userId, filterParam, filterValue } = ctx.request.url.searchParams;
  
  if (!userId || !filterParam) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "userId and filterParam are required" };
    return;
  }

  try {
    const key = ["users", userId, filterParam];
    
    if (filterValue) {
      const result = await kv.get<string>([...key, filterValue]);
      if (result.value) {
        ctx.response.body = { data: result.value };
      } else {
        ctx.response.status = 404;
        ctx.response.body = { error: "No data found" };
      }
    } else {
      const entries = [];
      for await (const entry of kv.list<string>({ prefix: key })) {
        entries.push({ key: entry.key, value: entry.value });
      }
      ctx.response.body = { data: entries };
    }
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

router.post("/users", async (ctx) => {
  const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  const { userId, data } = body;
  
  if (!userId || !data) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "userId and data are required" };
    return;
  }

  try {
    const key = ["users", userId];
    await kv.set(key, data);
    ctx.response.body = { message: "Data stored successfully" };
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

router.put("/users/:userId", async (ctx) => {
  const { userId } = ctx.params;
  const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  const { data } = body;
  
  if (!userId || !data) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "userId and data are required" };
    return;
  }

  try {
    const key = ["users", userId];
    await kv.set(key, data);
    ctx.response.body = { message: "Data updated successfully" };
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

router.delete("/users/:userId", async (ctx) => {
  const { userId } = ctx.params;
  
  if (!userId) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "userId is required" };
    return;
  }

  try {
    const key = ["users", userId];
    await kv.delete(key);
    ctx.response.body = { message: "Data deleted successfully" };
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

const app = new Application();
app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

app.addEventListener("listen", (evt) => {
  console.log(`Listening on http://${evt.hostname}:${evt.port}/`);
});

await app.listen({ port: 8000 });
